title: Open when anxiety gets loud
preview: A slower page for the moments your chest forgets there is room.
body: Put one hand somewhere warm. The blanket, your sleeve, the side of your face. You do not need to solve the entire future from this minute.
The exam is important. So are you. Both can be true without one swallowing the other.
For now, do the smallest kind thing: drink water, unclench your jaw, and let the next breath arrive before the next thought.

title: Open when home feels too quiet
preview: For evenings when everyone feels far away.
body: This room has seen how hard you are trying. It has seen the notes, the pauses, the quiet tears, and the way you keep coming back anyway.
You are not hard to love when you are tired. You are not behind in being human.
Somewhere, someone is rooting for the version of you who just needs company, not advice.
tinyAction: Send a one-word message to someone safe.

title: Open when everything feels too much
preview: A softer place to set the whole mountain down.
body: You do not have to carry all 90 days at once. Today can be held in your hands. Just today.
Pick the next gentle unit: one topic, one block, one glass of water, one five-minute reset.
There is no prize for panicking perfectly. Come back to the next possible thing.
tinyAction: Write the next three tasks. Cross out two.

title: Open after a bad mock test
preview: For when a number tries to tell the whole story.
body: A mock is a map with a red circle on it. It is not a verdict. It is not your worth wearing a score.
Let it sting, then let it teach. The useful part is smaller than the shame wants you to believe.
You are allowed to be upset and still return tomorrow with a clearer plan.
tinyAction: Find only one pattern from the mistakes.

title: Open when you are exhausted
preview: A permission slip for the body that has carried you.
body: Your body is not betraying you by needing rest. It is trying to keep the lights on.
If you cannot study deeply, study gently. If you cannot study gently, recover honestly.
Rest is not a crack in discipline. It is part of staying here long enough to finish.
tinyAction: Take a real ten-minute break, without earning it.

title: Open when focus will not come
preview: For the foggy days when the page keeps sliding away.
body: Focus is not a personality trait. It is weather. Some days you work with drizzle.
Make the room smaller: one tab, one notebook, one timer, one question.
You can restart without dramatizing the restart. That counts too.
tinyAction: Set a 12-minute timer and begin badly.

title: Open when you want to quit
preview: Not a lecture. Just a hand on the shoulder.
body: Of course a part of you wants to quit. That part has been working without enough softness for a long time.
Listen to it. Feed it. Let it sleep. But do not let one exhausted hour make decisions for the whole brave year.
You can pause. You can cry. You can lower the volume. Then, when you are ready, you can take one step.
tinyAction: Do nothing for five minutes except breathe.
